name: Embalse de Mansilla – circular by Viniegra de Abajo and Mansilla de la Sierra – Montes de Urbión
name_original: Embalse de Mansilla – circular por Viniegra de Abajo y Mansilla de la Sierra – Montes de Urbión
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Viniegra de Abajo are situated in La Rioja, Spain, nestled between the Camero Nuevo and Sierra de la Demanda mountains. The region features varied terrain, including the Urbión river valley, forests of oaks and holm oaks, and the nearby Embalse de Mansilla. This area offers a picturesque setting for exploration, characterized by its natural landscapes and winding paths. Best touring cycling routes around Viniegra De Abajo The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Mansilla – circular by Viniegra de Abajo and Mansilla de la Sierra – Montes de Urbión, an 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular journey around the Mansilla Reservoir, passing through the Montes de Urbión. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Molinos de Duero – Laguna Negra Ascent. loop from Covaleda, a difficult 38.9 miles (62.6 km) path. This route features a significant ascent towards Laguna Negra, starting and ending near Covaleda. Local touring cyclists also love the Hostal Restaurante Torreblanca – Duero Trail loop from Covaleda, a 12.9 miles (20.8 km) trail leading through the Duero Trail, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Viniegra De Abajo is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and diverse forests, including oaks and holm oaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Viniegra De Abajo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 90 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Viniegra De Abajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Viniegra de Abajo?
A: There are 12 touring cycling routes around Viniegra de Abajo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 5 moderate routes and 7 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Viniegra de Abajo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Viniegra de Abajo are characterized by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and diverse forests, including oaks and holm oaks. You'll encounter winding paths and varied landscapes, from the Urbión river valley to the Sierra de la Demanda mountains.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Viniegra de Abajo?
A: Viniegra de Abajo offers beautiful cycling conditions, particularly during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the higher elevations, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Viniegra de Abajo?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy' in our current selection, there are 5 routes classified as 'moderate'. These routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostal Restaurante Torreblanca – Duero Trail loop from Covaleda&lt;/a&gt;, offer less demanding ascents and are a good starting point for those with some cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenges. There are 7 routes graded as 'difficult', featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Duero – Laguna Negra Ascent. loop from Covaleda&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a notable climb towards Laguna Negra.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994455653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Mansilla – circular by Viniegra de Abajo and Mansilla de la Sierra – Montes de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you around the scenic Mansilla Reservoir.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by natural attractions like waterfalls or lakes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find routes that lead to stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt;. The Urbión river valley itself offers picturesque scenery, and the source of the Duero river is also nearby the Pico de Urbión.

Q: What points of interest can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, Viniegra de Abajo is one of 'the most beautiful villages in Spain'. You can explore its charming urban center with cobbled streets, the distinctive 'Casas de los Indianos', and historic sites like the Parish Church of La Asunción and the medieval Puente Canto. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Viniegra de Abajo?
A: Viniegra de Abajo, being a small village, typically offers parking options within or on the outskirts of the village center. For routes starting from nearby towns like Covaleda, designated parking areas are usually available. It's always a good idea to check specific route details or local signage upon arrival.

Q: How can I reach Viniegra de Abajo using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Viniegra de Abajo can be limited due to its rural location. While there might be bus services connecting to larger towns in La Rioja, direct routes to Viniegra de Abajo are less frequent. It's recommended to check regional bus schedules in advance or consider driving to the area.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Viniegra de Abajo and surrounding villages offer local amenities including restaurants and accommodation. You'll find traditional eateries where you can refuel after your ride. For specific recommendations, it's best to check local listings in Viniegra de Abajo or nearby towns like Covaleda.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Viniegra de Abajo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels, and the peaceful atmosphere of the region's forests and river valleys.
tips: The Mansilla reservoir is located on the Najerilla river, a tributary of the Ebro river. Its waters hide the old town of Mansilla de la Sierra, whose church and some other buildings are visible when it is low.
The road continues favorably until Viniegra de Abajo, in the valley of the Urbión river, a beautiful set of popular architecture with stately homes and various Indian constructions.
